title: OSP Profile: Cold Chain Logistics v1
description: Vocabulary for temperature-controlled transport and storage services. Intended for pharmaceutical, food, and chemical logistics where temperature integrity during transit is decisive for service selection.

temperature_range_celsius.description: Supported temperature range as [min, max] in degrees Celsius. Example: [2, 8] for standard pharma cold chain.

temperature_precision_celsius.description: Monitoring resolution in degrees Celsius. Example: 0.5 means sensors record in 0.5°C steps.

temperature_logging.description: Frequency and model of temperature recording during transport.

excursion_policy.description: Provider's defined behaviour when the temperature leaves the declared range.

excursion_notification_time.description: Maximum time from excursion detection to customer notification. Example: '< 15 minutes'.

qualifications.description: Applicable cold chain qualification standards the provider operates under.

qualified_packaging_available.description: Whether the provider offers qualified / validated passive packaging (e.g., PCM, VIP).

packaging_types.description: Supported thermal packaging or conditioning methods.

mean_kinetic_temperature_reported.description: Whether the provider calculates and reports MKT per shipment, as expected under USP <1079>.

temperature_log_format.description: Format in which the temperature record is delivered to the customer.

qualified_drivers.description: Whether drivers receive formal cold-chain handling training and this is documented.
